sections.paragraph: f. [pl. with suff. מֶגְרְפֹותֵיהֶם], a clod of earth, which is removed with a spade or other like instrument; prop. ein Spatenftich, (from the root גָּרַף). Joe 1:17, “the grains are dried up under their clods,” by which words the utmost drought is described. So Aben Ezra and Kimchi. (Syr. ܡܰܓܪܰܦܬܐܳ, مِغْرَفَةُ a spade itself.)
